complete_a_challenge_using_fewer_than_: Complete a challenge using fewer than 5 blocks
use_random_block_in_a_repeat_loop: Use Random block in a Repeat loop
add_a_move_forward_block_and_run_it: Add a "Move Forward" block and run it.
drag_a_move_forward_block_to_your_prog: Drag a Move Forward block to your program and click Run!
draw_a_square_using_move_and_turn_bloc: Draw a square using Move and Turn blocks.
you_need_4_move_forward_4_turn_right_9: You need 4× Move Forward + 4× Turn Right 90°
draw_the_same_square_using_a_repeat_bl: Draw the same square using a Repeat block.
use_repeat_4_with_move_forward_and_tur: Use Repeat 4× with Move Forward and Turn Right 90° inside.
draw_3_lines_each_a_different_color: Draw 3+ lines, each a different color.
use_set_color_blocks_between_your_move: Use Set Color blocks between your Move Forward blocks.
create_a_spiral_that_grows_outward: Create a spiral that grows outward.
this_is_tricky_try_increasing_the_dist: This is tricky! Try increasing the distance each time.
create_any_drawing_with_20_line_segmen: Create any drawing with 20+ line segments.
draw_a_house_a_square_base_with_a_tria: Draw a house: a square base with a triangle roof on top.
draw_a_square_then_use_pen_up_to_move_: Draw a square, then use Pen Up to move, then draw a triangle for the roof. Think about angles: square = 90°, triangle = 120°.
study_this_finished_square_then_click_: Study this finished square, then click Run to watch it draw.
notice_the_pattern_move_turn_repeated_: Notice the pattern: move, turn, repeated four times.
this_loop_should_draw_a_square_but_it_: This loop should draw a square but it is missing the turn. Add a Turn Right 90 inside the Repeat.
add_turn_right_90_after_the_move_forwa: Add Turn Right 90 after the Move Forward, inside the Repeat block.
a_finished_triangle_made_with_a_loop_r: A finished triangle made with a loop. Run it, then read how it works.
repeat_three_times_move_forward_then_t: Repeat three times: move forward, then turn 120.
almost_a_hexagon_add_the_missing_turn_: Almost a hexagon. Add the missing turn inside the loop to close it.
add_turn_right_60_after_the_move_forwa: Add Turn Right 60 after the Move Forward, inside the Repeat block.
check_if_a_wall_is_in_front: Check if a wall is in front
check_if_standing_on_a_gem: Check if standing on a gem
pick_up_the_gem_at_current_position: Pick up the gem at current position
move_the_robot_forward_3_spaces_to_rea: Move the robot forward 3 spaces to reach the goal!
navigate_around_the_corner_to_reach_th: Navigate around the corner to reach the goal.
move_forward_then_turn_then_move_forwa: Move forward, then turn, then move forward again.
use_a_repeat_block_to_reach_the_goal_e: Use a Repeat block to reach the goal efficiently.
move_through_the_path_and_collect_all_: Move through the path and collect all 3 gems!
move_to_each_gem_and_use_collect_gem_o: Move to each gem and use "Collect Gem" on each one.
use_if_wall_ahead_to_navigate_a_maze_w: Use "If Wall Ahead" to navigate a maze with turns!
combine_while_not_at_goal_with_if_wall: Combine "While Not At Goal" with "If Wall Ahead" → Turn Right, else → Move Forward.
paint_all_the_unpainted_cells_in_the_r: Paint all the unpainted cells in the row!
use_repeat_with_move_forward_and_paint: Use Repeat with "Move Forward" and "Paint Cell".
navigate_the_spiral_maze_using_loops_a: Navigate the spiral maze using loops and conditionals!
try_while_not_at_goal_if_wall_turn_rig: Try "While Not At Goal": If Wall → Turn Right, else → Move Forward.
collect_all_gems_and_reach_the_goal_us: Collect ALL gems and reach the goal! Use everything you learned.
plan_your_path_carefully_you_may_need_: Plan your path carefully. You may need nested loops!
add_coding_blocks_from_this_toolbox_to: Add coding blocks from this Toolbox to build your program.
each_block_is_a_command_set_values_wit: Each block is a command. Set values with the number inputs. Drag blocks to reorder!
click_run_to_watch_your_program_execut: Click Run to watch your program execute step by step on the canvas.
try_challenges_to_learn_cs_concepts_li: Try Challenges to learn CS concepts like loops and earn XP! 🏆
switch_to_code_mode_for_javascript_lik: Switch to Code mode for JavaScript-like syntax. Everything stays in sync!
sub_millisecond_imperceptible_to_user_: Sub-millisecond. Imperceptible to user. Safe for hot inner loops.
1_ms_well_within_frame_rate_budget_saf: < 1 ms. Well within frame-rate budget; safe for UI event handlers.
1ms_1s_will_visibly_lag_ui_needs_progr: 1ms–1s. Will visibly lag UI; needs progress bar or move off main thread.
seconds_to_minutes_batch_job_territory: Seconds to minutes. Batch-job territory; user gets coffee.
practically_infinite_at_this_scale_nee: Practically infinite at this scale. Need a better algorithm or smaller input.
set_input_size_loop_depth_data_structu: Set input size, loop depth, data structure, and recursion shape. Predict how fast (or slow) your code will run before testing it. No score, no reveal.
your_hypothesis_which_change_buys_the_: Your hypothesis (which change buys the biggest speedup — smaller n, fewer loops, or different data structure?)
e_g_swapping_array_scan_for_hash_looku: e.g., swapping array scan for hash lookup drops outer-loop cost by a factor of n...
why_does_loop_depth_multiply_rather_th: Why does loop depth multiply rather than add to operation count?
when_does_it_matter_that_hash_lookup_i: When does it matter that hash lookup is O(1) — at n=10? n=1,000? n=1,000,000?
exponential_recursion_explodes_after_r: Exponential recursion explodes after roughly what n? Where exactly does the wall hit?
for_very_small_n_sometimes_o_n_beats_o: For very small n, sometimes O(n²) BEATS O(n log n). Why?
i_can_explain_why_this_combination_of_: I can explain why this combination of n, depth, data structure, and recursion lands here.
inquiry_widget_no_score_no_reveal_no_a: Inquiry widget — no score, no reveal, no answer dump. Ops counts are illustrative pedagogical estimates assuming ~1 GHz effective throughput; real performance depends on cache, branch prediction, JIT, and constants in front of the Big-O term.
ai_explains_what_your_code_does: AI explains what your code does
ai_suggests_what_to_try_next: AI suggests what to try next
ai_helps_debug_your_challenge_attempt: AI helps debug your challenge attempt
move_forward_turn_pen_down_a_few_primi: Move forward, turn, pen down: a few primitives → fractals, spirals, polygons. Seymour Papert’s Mindstorms (1980) showed kids could BUILD intuition for math by teaching the turtle, not the other way round.
robot_navigate_the_grid_collect_solve: Robot — navigate the grid, collect, solve
sequencing_conditionals_loops_function: Sequencing → conditionals → loops → functions, wrapped in a maze you can SEE. Mirrors Karel the Robot (Stanford 1981) and modern code.org puzzles. Errors become spatial: “it walked into a wall.”
drag_commands_into_your_program_use_if: 💡 Drag commands into your program. Use If/While blocks for smart navigation!
click_commands_from_the_toolbox_to_bui: Click commands from the toolbox to build your program!
csta_k_12_iste_ct_sequencing_loops_con: CSTA K-12 • ISTE CT • Sequencing, Loops, Conditionals, Algorithms
click_blocks_above_or_load_a_template_: Click blocks above or load a template to start
forward_50_right_90_backward_30_repeat:
forward(50)
right(90)
backward(30)

repeat(4, function() {
  forward(100)
  right(90)
})
commands_forward_n_backward_n_right_de: Commands: forward(n) backward(n) right(deg) left(deg) penUp() penDown() setColor("#hex") setWidth(n) circle(r) goto(x,y) home() arc(angle,radius) stamp() playNote(freq,dur). Variables: setVar("name",value) changeVar("name",delta) random("name",min,max) — use a variable with $ (e.g. forward($name)). Loops & logic use braces: repeat(n){ ... }  while (x < 450){ ... }  if (x > 250){ ... } else { ... }. Functions: function myShape(){ ... } then call it with myShape(). Conditions compare x, y, angle, or $var using > < >= <= == != .
